headers.//: Vite content-hashed bundles — safe to cache forever (filename changes when content changes).

Everything else (HTML, /, all SPA routes that rewrite to index.html, sw.js, /pricing, /book-now, /dashboard, etc.) → never cached. The moment a new deploy lands, every browser fetches the new HTML on next navigation, which references the new asset hashes, which loads the fresh JS. Without this, the SPA rewrite to /index.html was inheriting the rewrite source's headers — meaning /book-now was caching the old HTML for an arbitrary duration depending on Vercel CDN.
